title: Drop the enforced-but-undeclared drift direction
description:
Enforcement-drift currently reports two directions; the `enforced-but-undeclared` direction unions rule names and gate keys indiscriminately (drift.rs:352-359) and treats every rule and gate as something an invariant must claim. On the seed registry this produces ~18 findings (11 gate + 7 rule subjects), forcing `jit invariant check` to exit non-zero (has_drift in commands/invariant.rs). The owner decided this nag has no value.

Scope: remove the `enforced-but-undeclared` direction entirely from the drift computation (no rule or gate subject reported), leaving `declared-but-unenforced` (an invariants `enforced-by` resolving to nothing) as the SOLE error direction. Update `DriftFinding`/`DriftDirection` (drift.rs:51-127) if the variant is removed, the `commands/invariant.rs` reporting + `has_drift` exit path, and the affected drift unit tests. The seed`s declared-but-unenforced direction is already empty (all four bindings resolve), so check exits 0.

## Success Criteria
- [hard] LR-05: Enforcement-drift emits no enforced-but-undeclared finding for any subject (rule or gate); declared-but-unenforced is the only error direction.
- [hard] LR-05b: `jit invariant check` exits 0 on the seed registry, and exits non-zero when an invariant names an unresolvable `enforced-by`.
